svg.title: Low tax versus banking friction
svg.desc: A two-by-two quadrant. The horizontal axis is tax level, from low on the left to high on the right. The vertical axis is banking friction, from low at the bottom to high at the top. The point of the chart is that low tax is not the same as low risk: a jurisdiction can have low headline tax and high banking friction, where moving money cleanly is hard. The comfortable quadrant is low tax with low friction; the deceptive one is low tax with high friction. No countries are plotted.
svg.text: Low tax is not the same as low risk
A low headline rate means little if you cannot move money cleanly and legally.
